checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueServicesNet=114,413,105.33 (Q3 2013) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=130,897,530.27 (Q3 2014), a 14.4% YoY increase

AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=95,730,276.54 (2013-06-30) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=124,239,850.41 (2014-06-30), 29.8% growth, while SalesRevenueServicesNet grew only from 111,498,718.59 (Q2 2013) to 126,555,982.99 (Q2 2014), 13.5%

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueServicesNet=164,517,492.11 (2010 annual) growing to SalesRevenueServicesNet=371,234,727.66 (2012 annual), a ~126% increase, well below goodwill growth over the same period

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=132,053,193.94 (2011 annual) / SalesRevenueServicesNet=252,134,096.74 (2011 annual) = 52.4% margin; GrossProfit=249,719,456.44 (2013 annual) / SalesRevenueServicesNet=453,889,907.19 (2013 annual) = 55.0% margin

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 50,471,988.74 (2011-06-30) to 79,822,482.12 (2012-09-30) while the allowance remained flat at 245,067.97, dropping the reserve ratio from ~0.49% to ~0.31% of receivables

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 8-K/A filed 2012-03-05 and 8-K/A filed 2012-10-24, consistent with routine pro forma business combination disclosures; no NT 10-K or NT 10-Q filings present in the chronology

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueServicesNet=134,347,702.87 (2009 annual) through AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=125,383,500.94 (2014-09-30) provide multi-year, multi-quarter coverage

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature revenue recognition, extended/loosened customer credit terms, or channel stuffing causing receivables to build faster than sales; DSO expanded from roughly 78 days (Q3 2013) to roughly 87-89 days (Q2-Q3 2014).

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=97,681,209.79 (2013-09-30) vs 125,383,500.94 (2014-09-30) = 28.4% growth vs SalesRevenueServicesNet growth of only 14.4% (114,413,105.33 to 130,897,530.27, Q3 2013 vs Q3 2014)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Understated bad debt reserve/allowance relative to a rapidly growing receivables base, potentially understating bad debt expense and overstating net receivables and net income during 2011-2012.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=245,067.97 unchanged across six consecutive quarters (2011-06-30 through 2012-09-30) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 50,471,988.74 to 79,822,482.12 over the same period

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Aggressive purchase price allocation in multiple acquisitions (evidenced by 8-K/A pro forma filings) pushing more value into goodwill relative to identifiable intangibles/organic growth, creating future impairment risk not yet reflected.

rationale_evidence.quote: Goodwill=42,372,732.58 (2010-12-31) grew to Goodwill=158,152,932.77 (2012-12-31), ~273% increase, versus SalesRevenueServicesNet growth of only ~126% over the same period (164,517,492.11 to 371,234,727.66)

top_signals: Accounts receivable growing 2x faster than revenue in 2014 quarters (28-30% AR growth vs 13-14% revenue growth), indicating expanding DSO
Allowance for doubtful accounts held completely flat ($245,067.97) for six consecutive quarters in 2011-2012 while receivables grew ~58%, sharply reducing reserve coverage ratio
Goodwill grew ~273% from 2010 to 2012 versus ~126% revenue growth over the same period, reflecting an active roll-up strategy with elevated future impairment risk
Operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income across 2011-2013, which is a mitigating (not aggravating) factor for earnings quality
No late-filing (NT) notices or restatements observed in the filing chronology, and 8-K/A and 10-K/A amendments appear routine/explainable
